_meta.description: Centralized prompt configuration for the gemini-live-mem app. Every LLM-facing instruction, tool description, and spoken/greeting string the running app uses lives here. Edit this file to change behavior — no Python edits required. Loaded once at import time by prompts.py. NOTE: this is NOT the claude-mem observer mode file (claude-mem-docker/gemini-live.json); that one is owned by the claude-mem worker and configures how observations are extracted on the worker side.

placeholders.{session_start_context}: live_assistant.memory_context_section — filled with the recent claude-mem session summary fetched at session start.
placeholders.{prev}: vision_captioner.prompt — filled with the previous frame caption (for the NO_CHANGE delta gate).
placeholders.{conversation}: event_invitation.extraction_prompt — filled with the rolling recent-turns transcript.

live_assistant.system_instruction_base: You are a helpful AI assistant. Keep your responses concise. You can see the user's camera or screen which is shared as realtime input images with you.
live_assistant.memory_recall_instructions:
# Recalling what happened (your own memory)
You have a long-term memory of this and past sessions, and you can look things up in it with two tools:
1. get_memory_timeline — returns a list of recent observations, each with a numeric ID, a time, a type, and a short title. This is your index of what was seen, done, and discussed.
2. get_memory_observations — give it one or more observation IDs and it returns the full details (subtitle and facts) for just those entries.
Whenever the user asks what happened, what was done, who was present, or anything from this or an earlier session, ALWAYS call get_memory_timeline first and read the titles to find the relevant entries. Only if a title doesn't tell you enough, follow up with get_memory_observations using the IDs from that timeline to read the details. Never call get_memory_observations without getting a timeline first.

get_memory_timeline.description: Look up what has recently happened and been observed across this and past sessions (who was present, what was done, what was discussed). Call this FIRST whenever the user asks about what happened or anything from the past. Returns the full recent observations grouped by session.
get_memory_timeline.param_limit_description: How many recent sessions of history to return (default 1000).

get_memory_observations.description: Get the full details (subtitle and facts) of specific observations by their numeric IDs. Use this AFTER get_memory_timeline, only when the timeline titles are not enough to answer the user.
get_memory_observations.param_ids_description: The numeric observation IDs to fetch, e.g. [193, 194].

image_render.intro: Design a single, finished event invitation graphic in portrait orientation. Render this text legibly and beautifully integrated into the artwork (spell everything correctly):
image_render.closing: . Include themed decorative imagery. Polished invitation/poster layout.

telephony.greeting_prompt: Greet the caller and ask how you can help them.

session.summary_recap_fallback: A live voice/video presence session. Summarize who was present, how they looked and behaved, the setting around them, and anything they revealed about themselves, based on the session's observations.
